steps.title: Find vertex x-coordinate
steps.content: A horizontal line intersects a parabola exactly once only when it is tangent at the vertex. Graph the parabola and the four horizontal answer choices in Desmos. The line that just touches the parabola is $y=-\frac{255}{4}$.

steps.title: Evaluate parabola at vertex
steps.content:
Substitute $x = \frac{7}{2}$ into $y = -x^2 + 7x - 76$:
$$y = -\left(\frac{7}{2}\right)^2 + 7\left(\frac{7}{2}\right) - 76 = -\frac{49}{4} + \frac{49}{2} - 76$$
Convert to a common denominator of 4:
$$= -\frac{49}{4} + \frac{98}{4} - \frac{304}{4} = \frac{-255}{4}$$
Therefore $c = -\frac{255}{4}$. That confirms D is correct.
